newsSince: No new June 9-23 order/contract/guidance news. The structural HBM4/2nm test narrative (70% capacity expansion in 2026, 10,000 systems/yr by 2028, FY-Mar-2027 guidance ~+25% sales) all stem from the May fiscal-year report, which predates this window. The +19% move since June 9 is sector-flow/Nikkei AI re-rating, not a name-specific catalyst.

sources.label: Digitimes - Advantest ATE lead times tight on AI/HBM
Digitimes - Advantest beats on AI chip testing, cautious outlook
Advantest Q1-2026 earnings call - Alpha Spread

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold. Node criticality unchanged: near-monopoly AI/HBM SoC test (66% share, >6mo lead times, +70% capacity build) keeps Advantest a genuine bottleneck. But the +19.4% since June 9 is flow with no new order/guidance, so the bottleneck case is intact, not stronger.
verdicts.bull: AI/HBM test near-monopoly; HBM4/HBM4E stack-up multiplies test intensity; +25% FY27 sales guide; +70% capacity build.
verdicts.neutral: Genuine bottleneck but well-discovered; recent move is sector flow.
verdicts.bear: ATE is cyclical; cautious outlook dented shares on results; export-control risk to China.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: HBM4/HBM4E ramp and tester capacity additions
verdicts.primaryRisk: ATE cycle turn and China export controls

verdicts.rankRationale: Down slightly. On a CPO-specific lens the optical/advanced-package test angle is secondary, and a +19.4% flow-driven run since June 9 on zero CPO news erodes the little residual asymmetry rank 45 had. The page's own risk - high AI expectations - just got more expensive.
verdicts.bull: Optical/advanced-package test expands as CPO scales; test leadership extends into co-packaged optics.
verdicts.neutral: CPO is a peripheral revenue lever vs core SoC/HBM test.
verdicts.bear: No CPO-specific catalyst; rich valuation amplifies disappointment risk on a CPO-purity lens.
verdicts.primaryRisk: High AI expectations; CPO is secondary to the thesis

verdicts.rankRationale: Down. This page rewards bottleneck exposure that has NOT already rerated; +19.4% since June 9 with YTD now +48.4% and no new evidence keeps narrowing the discovery discount. The pick-and-shovel thesis is real but increasingly priced.
verdicts.bull: Best pure pick-and-shovel on AI test; durable pricing power and share gains.
verdicts.neutral: Still a quality bottleneck, just less under-owned.
verdicts.bear: +48% YTD with no fresh catalyst shrinks the under-owned-bottleneck premium this page targets.
